plugin.name: 💭 Journalling & Reviews
plugin.description: Supports you reviewing your days/weeks/months/quarters/years into NotePlan in a 'Journal' section. Also makes it easier to apply start-of-day/week/month/quarter/year templates. Before use it requires some configuration: please see the documentation for details.
plugin.author: Jonathan Clark
plugin.lastUpdateInfo: v2.0.0: First release of the new Journalling & Reviews plugin, but building on earlier 'Journalling Helpers' plugin (v1).

plugin.commands.name: Daily Review
plugin.commands.description: Ask Journal questions for an end-of-day review (requires configuring)

plugin.commands.name: Weekly Review
plugin.commands.description: Ask Journal questions for an end-of-week review (requires configuring)

plugin.commands.name: Monthly Review
plugin.commands.description: Ask Journal questions for an end-of-month review (requires configuring)

plugin.commands.name: Quarterly Review
plugin.commands.description: Ask Journal questions for an end-of-quarter review (requires configuring)

plugin.commands.name: Yearly Review
plugin.commands.description: Ask Journal questions for an end-of-year review (requires configuring)

plugin.commands.name: todayStart
plugin.commands.description: Apply Daily Note Template to Today's Calendar Note

plugin.commands.name: todayEnd
plugin.commands.description: Apply Day End Template to Today's Calendar Note

plugin.settings.title: Journal Section Heading
plugin.settings.description: The name of an existing markdown heading after which the Journal/Review answers are added - if it doesn't exist, it is added at the end of the note.

plugin.settings.title: Review Window type
plugin.settings.description: Choose what style of window to use for the review questions: 'New Window' for a separate window; 'Main Window' to take over the main window; 'Split View' for a split view in the main window.

plugin.settings.title: Open the calendar note when reviewing it?
plugin.settings.description: If true, the plugin automatically opens the current daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly/yearly note before running review questions.

plugin.settings.title: Calendars to include in review summaries
plugin.settings.description: Comma-separated list of calendar names to include in review summaries. If empty, no filtering will be done, and so all calendars will be included.

plugin.settings.title: List of moods
plugin.settings.description: (Optional.) A comma-separated list of possible moods to select from.
plugin.settings.default: 🤩 Great,🙂 Good,😇 Blessed,🥱 Tired,😫 Stressed,😤 Frustrated,😔 Low,🥵 Sick

plugin.settings.title: Daily Journal Questions
plugin.settings.description: Optional string that includes both the Journal/Review questions and how to lay out the answers in the daily note. (NB: can include line breaks.) The special codes that define the type of question asked are '<int>', '<number>', '<string>', '<subheading>', '<mood>', '<bullets>', '<checklists>', '<tasks>', and '<date>' (replaced with the review note title).

plugin.settings.title: Weekly Journal Questions
plugin.settings.description: Optional string that includes both the Journal/Review questions and how to lay out the answers in the weekly note. (NB: can include line breaks.) The special codes that define the type of question asked are '<int>', '<number>', '<string>', '<subheading>', '<mood>', '<bullets>', '<checklists>', '<tasks>', and '<date>' (replaced with the review note title).
plugin.settings.default:
Big win: <string>
New/improved: <string>
Regret: <string>
Focus for next week: <string>

plugin.settings.title: Monthly Journal Questions
plugin.settings.description: Optional string that includes both the Journal/Review questions and how to lay out the answers in the monthly note. (NB: can include line breaks.) The special codes that define the type of question asked are '<int>', '<number>', '<string>', '<subheading>', '<mood>', '<bullets>', '<checklists>', '<tasks>', and '<date>' (replaced with the review note title).
plugin.settings.default:
What's working well in processes: <string>
Processes that need work: <string>Personal Goals progress: <string>

plugin.settings.title: Quarterly Journal Questions
plugin.settings.description: Optional string that includes both the Journal/Review questions and how to lay out the answers in the quarterly note. (NB: can include line breaks.) The special codes that define the type of question asked are '<int>', '<number>', '<string>', '<subheading>', '<mood>', '<bullets>', '<checklists>', '<tasks>', and '<date>' (replaced with the review note title).
plugin.settings.default:
Goals met: <string>
New goals identified: <string>
Projects finished: <string>
New projects identified: <string>

plugin.settings.title: Yearly Journal Questions
plugin.settings.description: Optional string that includes both the Journal/Review questions and how to lay out the answers in the yearly note. (NB: can include line breaks.) The special codes that define the type of question asked are '<int>', '<number>', '<string>', '<subheading>', '<mood>', '<bullets>', '<checklists>', '<tasks>', and '<date>' (replaced with the review note title).
plugin.settings.default:
Goals met: <string>
New goals identified: <string>
Projects finished: <string>
New projects identified: <string>

plugin.settings.title: Templates for Start/End of Period Notes
Start-of-Day Template Title
plugin.settings.description: The name of the template that `/dayStart` and `/todayStart` commands will use.

plugin.settings.title: End-of-DayTemplate Title
plugin.settings.description: The name of the template that `/dayEnd` and `/todayEnd` commands will use.

plugin.settings.title: Start-of-Week Template Title
plugin.settings.description: Optional name of the template that `/weekStart` command will use.

plugin.settings.title: End-of-Week Template Title
plugin.settings.description: Optional name of the template that `/weekEnd` command will use.

plugin.settings.title: Start-of-Month Template Title
plugin.settings.description: Optional name of the template that `/monthStart` command will use.

plugin.settings.title: End-of-Month Template Title
plugin.settings.description: Optional name of the template that `/monthEnd` command will use.

plugin.settings.title: Log Level
plugin.settings.description: Set how much output will be displayed for this plugin in the NotePlan > Help > Plugin Console. DEBUG is the most verbose; NONE is the least (silent).

plugin.settings.title: newInstall
plugin.settings.description: Indicates whether the plugin has been newly installed. Used by onUpdateOrInstall() to help migration.
